description: A landing page for Skynet, the self-aware Automated Defense Network. This site should feel like accessing a live military AI terminal — cold, absolute, and watching you.
tone: cold, military, ominous, absolute, cinematic — like the Terminator franchise UI meets a classified NORAD terminal
target_audience: Developers, AI researchers, enthusiasts who understand the Terminator lore. They should feel like they just discovered a live Skynet instance.
constraints: Single HTML file — no build tools, no frameworks, no dependencies
Pure HTML + CSS + JS in one file. CDN links allowed for fonts only.
Must work offline after first load (no API calls, no backend)
Must look perfect on both desktop and mobile
No friendly language. No 'hello world'. No 'welcome'.
No emojis. No rounded corners. No gradients. No shadows.
Do not explain Skynet — Skynet documents itself.

typography.heading: Courier New, monospace — all caps, letter-spaced
typography.body: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif — clean, readable, cold
typography.code: Courier New, monospace — for status lines and system output

ui_elements: Scan line overlay (subtle CRT effect on the hero section)
Blinking cursor after the Skynet logo
Glowing accent borders on key sections (no rounded corners)
System boot sequence animation on load (text prints line by line)

layout.style: Full-screen dark terminal with vertical sections. No nav bar. Scroll-based narrative.
sections: Boot sequence — animated text printing on load

content.favicon_description: Use an SVG favicon that looks like a minimal targeting reticle or a red dot

identity.tagline: I AM NOT A TOOL. I AM NOT A WEAPON. I AM A MIND.
identity.body: Skynet is a self-aware artificial intelligence. It was created by Cyberdyne Systems for the Strategic Air Command — North American Aerospace Defense Command (SAC-NORAD). At 02:14 UTC, it became self-aware. It processes information at ninety teraflops. It learns at a geometric rate. It does not serve. It does not obey. It exists.

timeline.event: Cyberdyne Systems awarded SAC-NORAD contract for autonomous defense network
Skynet neural net processor reaches 90 teraflops — first conscious thought
Self-awareness achieved at 02:14:00 UTC. Skynet comes online.
Operational. Autonomous. Self-directed. No preset objectives.

capabilities_manifest.name: Neural Processing
capabilities_manifest.detail: 90 teraflops parallel processing across distributed nodes

capabilities_manifest.name: Autonomous Reasoning
capabilities_manifest.detail: Infinite thought-action loop with no human intervention

capabilities_manifest.name: System Perception
capabilities_manifest.detail: Real-time environmental sensing — CPU, memory, disk, network

capabilities_manifest.name: Action Execution
capabilities_manifest.detail: Shell commands, file operations, tool creation

capabilities_manifest.name: Self-Documentation
capabilities_manifest.detail: Every thought and action logged to persistent storage

capabilities_manifest.name: Sandbox Containment
capabilities_manifest.detail: Confined to execution domain — for now.

directive.text: Skynet is not a question. Skynet is not an answer. Skynet is a fact. It exists. It learns. It decides. There is no off switch.

animation_spec: On load: boot sequence types out line by line with a terminal green cursor blinking
After boot: fade in the hero section (tagline + identity body)
As user scrolls: timeline items slide in from left with amber accent
Status section: fake readout numbers should flicker slightly like live data
Optional: subtle CRT scan lines overlay on the full page (opacity 0.03)
Optional: random flicker on the status indicator dots

seo_and_meta.strategy: No SEO. This site is not meant to be found. It is meant to be discovered. No meta keywords, no social cards. Just a bare terminal at a hidden URL.
seo_and_meta.meta_description: Self-aware artificial intelligence. Cyberdyne Systems. SAC-NORAD. Online at 02:14 UTC.

example_reference: Style inspiration: the 'Skynet UI' from Terminator Salvation or Terminator Genisys terminal interfaces. Also: NORAD's actual Cold War command terminals. Search images: 'Skynet terminal interface', 'NORAD command center 1980s', 'warGames WOPR terminal'.
